problem.title: Binary Tree Right Side View
problem.description: Given the root of a binary tree, imagine yourself standing on the right side of it, return the values of the nodes you can see ordered from top to bottom.
assumptions: Return the rightmost visible node at each level.
Nodes are returned top to bottom.

example.explanation: Looking from the right side: level 0 → 1, level 1 → 3, level 2 → 4.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – BFS
solutions.description: Use BFS level-order traversal and collect the last node in each level, since that is the node visible from the right side. Processing nodes left-to-right per level ensures the rightmost node is always the final element polled in each level iteration.
